title: Heatwave persists across Odisha; thunderstorm alerts issued for several districts - Odisha TV
full_text:
The summer heat continues to grip Odisha, with intense daytime temperatures being felt across many parts of the state. While scorching conditions prevail during the day, several regions are also witnessing Nor'wester activity during the afternoon and evening hours.
India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a heatwave alert for several districts in western Odisha on Tuesday. Districts placed under heatwave warning include Sundargarh, Jharsuguda, Sambalpur, Angul, Dhenkanal, Bargarh, Sonepur, Boudh, Kandhamal, Bolangir, Kalahandi and Nuapada. Residents in these areas have been advised to take necessary precautions against prolonged exposure to high temperatures.
At the same time, the India Meteorological Department (IMD) has also issued thunderstorm warnings for more than half of the state's districts. A Yellow Warning has been sounded for all coastal districts as well as most districts in central Odisha, indicating the possibility of thunderstorms accompanied by lightning, gusty winds and light to moderate rainfall.

Meanwhile, Keonjhar and Mayurbhanj districts have been placed under an Orange Warning, signalling a higher likelihood of severe thunderstorm activity. Authorities have urged people in the likely affected areas to remain alert and follow weather advisories, particularly during the afternoon and evening hours when storm activity is expected to intensify.
The contrasting weather pattern of extreme heat during the day followed by thunderstorm activity later in the day has become a recurring feature across Odisha in recent weeks. Weather officials continue to closely monitor the evolving conditions and have advised the public to stay updated with the latest forecasts and warnings.
